title: Ensure that the CNI in use supports Network Policies

input_row.title: Ensure that the CNI in use supports Network Policies
input_row.description: There are a variety of CNI plugins available for Kubernetes. If the CNI in use does not support Network Policies it may not be possible to effectively restrict traffic in the cluster.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes network policies are enforced by the CNI plugin in use. As such it is important to ensure that the CNI plugin supports both Ingress and Egress network policies.
input_row.audit: Review the documentation of CNI plugin in use by the cluster, and confirm that it supports Ingress and Egress network policies.
input_row.remediation: If the CNI plugin in use does not support network policies, consideration should be given to making use of a different plugin, or finding an alternate mechanism for restricting traffic in the Kubernetes cluster.
input_row.default_value: This will depend on the CNI plugin in use.
input_row.additional_information: One example here is Flannel ( https://github.com/coreos/flannel ) which does not support Network policy unless Calico is also in use.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control requires checking whether the CNI plugin in use supports Network Policies. This information can be derived from the CNI plugin's documentation and configuration, which can be queried through Kubernetes API or kubectl commands.
- The audit mentions reviewing the documentation of the CNI plugin, which indicates that the information can be programmatically verified by checking the CNI configuration in the cluster.
- Kubernetes does expose properties related to network policies and CNI configurations, making this control suitable for an automated approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_network_policy_cni_support_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].name}'` to identify the CNI plugin in use by checking the pods in the kube-system namespace.
- Check the CNI configuration file typically located at `/etc/cni/net.d/` on the nodes, which can be accessed via a command like `kubectl exec -it <pod-name> -n kube-system -- cat /etc/cni/net.d/<cni-config-file>.conf` to retrieve the CNI configuration.
- Validate that the CNI plugin supports Network Policies by checking the documentation of the identified CNI plugin (e.g., Calico, Cilium, Weave Net) against known capabilities.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: The CNI plugin supports both Ingress and Egress network policies as per its documentation.
  - Fail: The CNI plugin does not support Network Policies or is not configured correctly to enforce them.
